en.extract:
The golden-browed warbler (Basileuterus belli) is a species of bird in the family Parulidae, the New World warblers.
It is found in El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, and Mexico.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
The species was described by Jacob Post Giraud, Jr. in 1841.


References


External links

Photo-High Res Archived 2011-07-25 at the Wayback Machine; Article mtaudubon–"Western Mexico"

The '''golden-browed warbler''' ('''''Basileuterus belli''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Parulidae]], the [[New World warbler]]s.
It is found in [[El Salvador]], [[Guatemala]], [[Honduras]], and [[Mexico]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
The species was described by Jacob Post Giraud, Jr. in 1841.<ref>[https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/15939789#page/574/mode/1up] {{aut|Wittmer Stone}} Jacob Post Giraud, Jr. and his works. The Auk, 1919]</ref>
